paper_title: External ballistics of Pleistocene hand-thrown spears: experimental performance data and implications for human evolution

methods.description: The process of setting up the experimental environment and acquiring data on spear throws using high-speed video cameras.

steps.description: Weather data accessed via Weather Underground website.
steps.searchable_summary: download weather data from Weather Underground

steps.description: Participants threw towards the northeast, in the direction of the wind, thus the wind provided a tailwind for the throws.
steps.searchable_summary: orient participants for optimal wind conditions

steps.description: Release throws and impacts were filmed using two HSV cameras with resolution at 800 × 600 at 1000 fps.
steps.searchable_summary: record throws using high-speed video cameras

steps.description: The camera for distance throws was positioned farther away than cameras for the target shots in order to widen the field of view.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust camera positions for optimal field of view

methods.description: The process of crafting spear replicas based on the Schöningen Spear II for experimental use.

steps.description: Spear replicas were crafted from Norwegian spruce trees grown on limestone/clay in Kent (UK).
steps.searchable_summary: craft spear replicas from Norwegian spruce

steps.description: Replicas were made by hand using metal tools, and the surface was worked at the final stage with lithic tools.
steps.searchable_summary: finish spear replicas with lithic tools

methods.description: The setup of targets for spear throwing experiments to assess accuracy and performance.

steps.description: A hay bale was placed horizontally on the ground at a series of distances from a throwing line for the aimed throws.
steps.searchable_summary: place hay bale targets at specified distances

steps.description: A series of additional throws took place with the hay bale turned vertically to assess whether the height of the target affected accuracy.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust target orientation to test accuracy

methods.description: The management and preparation of participants for the spear throwing experiments.

steps.description: Participants were orally briefed, provided signed informed consent, and were aware they could withdraw at any stage.
Participants were not permitted a run-up for aimed throws, but up to three steps were allowed to facilitate body position at release.
steps.searchable_summary: set participant movement restrictions for throws

methods.description: The analysis of high-speed video data to measure spear velocity, angles of release and impact, and spear performance.

steps.description: The software package Phantom Cine Viewer was used for HSV analysis to measure velocity, and angles of release and impact.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze HSV data for velocity and angles

steps.description: Standard and HSV were also analysed for spear flight paths and spear performance.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze spear flight paths and performance
